stem: Which of the following equations represents a circle in the xy-plane that intersects the y-axis at exactly one point?
rationale: An equation (x − h)2 + (y − k)2 = r2 describes a circle with center (h, k) and radius r. The circle intersects the y-axis at exactly one point exactly when the distance from the center to the y-axis equals the radius, i.e., |h| = r. Choice A has center (5, 7) and radius 5, so |h| = 5 = r. Choices B and C have |h| ≠ r; choice D has h = 0, so the circle crosses the y-axis at two points.
